isolation.notes: No runtime services needed — all checks are file-based

steps.observed: FROM oven/bun:1 AS deps — runs bun install --frozen-lockfile
FROM oven/bun:1 AS builder — runs bun run build

steps.expected: Node 22 Alpine runtime with standalone output
steps.observed: FROM node:22-alpine AS runner — copies .next/standalone and runs node server.js

evidence.fileContents: Dockerfile: 3 stages (deps, builder, runner). deps: oven/bun:1, bun install --frozen-lockfile. builder: oven/bun:1, bun run build. runner: node:22-alpine, copies standalone output, runs as nextjs user on port 3000.

steps.observed: File exists with app, postgres, cloudflared services

steps.expected: build from Dockerfile, env_file, depends_on postgres healthy
steps.observed: app: build context=., dockerfile=Dockerfile, env_file=.env.production, depends_on postgres condition=service_healthy
postgres: image postgres:16-alpine, healthcheck with pg_isready, volume pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data

evidence.fileContents: docker-compose.yml: 3 services (app, postgres, cloudflared). app builds from Dockerfile with env_file .env.production, depends_on postgres healthy. postgres uses postgres:16-alpine with pg_isready healthcheck and pgdata volume. cloudflared mounts /opt/inbound/cloudflared as config.

steps.observed: Line: output: "standalone" found in nextConfig object

assertions.title: Runtime content dirs copied in Dockerfile

evidence.fileContents: Dockerfile runner stage lines: COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public, COPY --from=builder /app/app/changelog/entries ./app/changelog/entries, COPY --from=builder /app/content/blog/images ./content/blog/images

steps.expected: Route checks DB and returns 200 when healthy
steps.observed: GET handler executes db.execute(sql`SELECT 1`), returns {status:'ok',db:'connected'} with 200 on success, {status:'error',db:'disconnected'} with 500 on failure

evidence.fileContents: app/api/health/route.ts: exports GET handler, tries db.execute(sql`SELECT 1`), returns 200 {status:'ok',db:'connected'} or 500 {status:'error',db:'disconnected'}

.env.example: All required env vars present — DATABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, INTERNAL_BASE_URL, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, CRON_SECRET, QSTASH_TOKEN, QSTASH_CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY, QSTASH_NEXT_SIGNING_KEY, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_REGION, S3_BUCKET_NAME, SERVICE_API_KEY, INBOUND_API_KEY, UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL, UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN, SVIX_API_KEY (optional), SUPPORT_EMAIL, LEGAL_EMAIL, USAGE_REPORT_TO. Includes sections: App, Database, Auth, Contact/Notifications, Redis, AWS, QStash, SDK, Cron/Webhooks, Optional.

steps.expected: Services share a Docker network (default or explicit)
steps.observed: All 3 services defined in same docker-compose.yml — they share the default network automatically

steps.action: Check cloudflared references app by service name
steps.observed: cloudflared has TUNNEL_URL: http://app:3000 — references app by Docker service name

evidence.fileContents: docker-compose.yml: No explicit network defined, so all services share the default network. cloudflared environment has TUNNEL_URL: http://app:3000, referencing app by Docker service name.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. Dockerfile has proper 3-stage build (deps/builder/runner), .dockerignore excludes required paths, docker-compose.yml defines all 3 services correctly, standalone output is enabled, runtime content dirs are copied, health endpoint checks DB connectivity, .env.example documents all required vars, and services share network with proper service-name references.
